channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Everything new on Hulu in January 2025
item.description: Hulu is gearing up to launching another load of movie and shows to the platform to kick off the new year. Here's everything new on Hulu in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Cómo trabaja Ikea con la IA: 30.000 empleados formados
item.description: Como la mayoría de las empresas, Ikea se centra en los datos y la inteligencia artificial (IA) para cumplir sus ambiciones de futuro. Por ello, hace cuatro años, la empresa desarrolló una estrategia de datos para todo el grupo, que se ha seguido desde entonces.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber
item.description: From Volt Typhoon to Salt Typhoon, major cyber incidents in 2024 shined a spotlight on how agencies are managing cyber threats to critical infrastructure. The post 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Crypto readies its wish list for Trump
item.description: The cryptocurrency industry spent big in the 2024 election, and its investment is about to pay off in the incoming Congress and administration.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Chinese competitor to AMD and Intel claims its 32-core processor is as powerful as a 2021 Intel Xeon 32-core 'Ice Lake' CPU; a 64-core version is expected to be launched in 2025
item.description: Loongson's 3C6000 series processors rival Intel Xeon chips, showcasing China's semiconductor progress.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: What is an IT consultant? Roles, types, salaries, and how to become one
item.description: The IT function within organizations has become far more complex in recent years. Enterprise infrastructures have expanded far beyond the traditional ones focused on company-owned and -operated data centers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: Finland’s prime minister says authorities are investigating an interruption in a power cable under the Baltic Sea between his country and Estonia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Snowy owl rescued from car grille by Minnesota woman who saved another bird hours earlier
item.description: A great gray owl and a snowy owl are being treated by experts after being rescued by a northern Minnesota woman.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple invests billions in Globalstar for satellite services and improved iPhone connectivity
item.description: Apple partnership with Globalstar challenges traditional telecom dominance with direct satellite communication.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Trump brings Silicon Valley to Washington
item.description: Silicon Valley is coming to Washington in force, as tech leaders take on prominent roles — both official and unofficial — in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cómo hablar con la junta directiva sobre la deuda tecnológica
item.description: Cuando su director ejecutivo o director financiero le pregunta sobre el presupuesto necesario para la remediación de la deuda técnica, ¿se encuentra con dificultades para justificar la inversión? No es el único.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Python in 2024: Faster, more powerful, and more popular than ever
item.description: Over the course of 2024, Python has proven again and again why it’s one of the most popular, useful, and promising programming languages out there.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok is full of bogus, potentially dangerous medical advice
item.description: TikTok is the new doctor’s office, quickly becoming a go-to platform for medical advice. Unfortunately, much of that advice is sketchy at best. A new report by the healthcare software firm Tebra found 45% of medical advice on TikTok to be false or misleading.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Timeshare terror leaves retired couple $50K in the hole after being scammed
item.description: The FBI is warning timeshare owners of a telemarketing scam tied to a Mexican drug cartel. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides steps to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The greatest keyboard never sold
item.description: Even as the latest phones and wearables tout speech recognition with unprecedented accuracy and spatial computing products flirt with replacing tablets and laptops, physical keyboards remain beloved for their efficiency. Earlier this year, for example, sci-fi novelist Robert J.
item.dc:creator: Ross Rubin, Fast Company

item.title: Baby mammoth preserved for 50,000 years is unveiled in Russia's Siberia
item.description: The 50,000-year-old remains of a baby mammoth uncovered by melting permafrost have been unveiled to the public by researchers in Russia’s Siberia region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Banning Chinese-made drones could hurt some Americans
item.description: Russell Hedrick, a North Carolina farmer, flies drones to spray fertilizers on his corn, soybean and wheat fields at a fraction of what it would cost him to use a conventional ground spreader.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: How (and why) to start using an RSS reader for news again
item.description: Imagine having a single app to keep up with your favorite news sources, YouTube channels, newsletters, and blogs, all in an easy-to-read format with no social media algorithms deciding what you see. That’s the idea behind RSS, a 25-year-old open standard for syndicating content from around the web.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Nissan and Honda to attempt a merger that would create the world's No. 3 automaker
item.description: Japanese automakers Nissan and Honda have announced plans to work toward a merger that would catapult them to a top position in an industry in the midst of tectonic shifts as it transitions away from its reliance on fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi And Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Albania bans TikTok for a year following death of teenager
item.description: Albania’s prime minister said the country will be banning TikTok for one year, citing violence and bullying among children after a teenager died.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Albania's prime minister said Saturday the government will shut down the video service TikTok for one year, blaming it for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why OMB’s human-centered policy design effort is paying off
item.description: Federal CIO Clare Martorana’s tenure is coming to an end in January and she reflects back on her office’s efforts to improve federal technology. The post Why OMB’s human-centered policy design effort is paying off first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: VA EHR to resume rollout in mid-2026, after clinicians see fewer crashes
item.description: The VA says clinicians are seeing fewer interruptions from the new EHR, and that the average user now sees “near zero interruptions,” such as freezes or lags. The post VA EHR to resume rollout in mid-2026, after clinicians see fewer crashes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Google’s contractors told to evaluate Gemini responses outside their expertise
item.description: Behind the responses from genAI models are testers who evaluate those answers for accuracy, but a report released this week casts doubt on the process.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
